description_long: This is a long, arched-roof barn with a corrugated metal exterior that shows signs of weathering and rust. The structure features a Quonset hut design, with a semi-cylindrical roof and vertical end walls. There are two large ventilation hatches or skylights on the roof, and multiple small rectangular windows are evenly spaced along both long sides. The front facade has a central entry door flanked by two windows, while the rear end features a large, barred opening, possibly for ventilation or livestock access. The barn's utilitarian appearance and robust construction suggest it is designed for agricultural storage or shelter, but its compact size could suit urban edge or peri-urban environments.
description: A weathered, arched-roof Quonset-style barn with corrugated metal siding, roof vents, and multiple small windows along its sides. The front features a central entry door, while the rear has a large barred opening.
description_view_0: Side view showing the corrugated metal siding, three evenly spaced windows, and two roof vents.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the arched end wall, central entry door, and two small windows on either side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first side with three windows and two roof vents visible.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a large barred opening and two small windows, with the arched roof and vents visible above.
attribute_door_style: single hinged door, centrally located on the front end wall
attribute_window_arrangement: three evenly spaced rectangular windows on each long side, two small windows flanking the front door, and two small windows on the rear end wall
